title: Quantum Lab
description: From-scratch quantum lab in TypeScript with FOUR simulation engines: state-vector, density-matrix, stabilizer-tableau (Clifford circuits to 30 qubits), and a Matrix Product State tensor network with a from-scratch complex SVD — running arbitrary circuits on 40 qubits in O(n·χ²) memory with error-tracked bond truncation, perfect sampling and per-cut entanglement entropy. On top of the tensor network: TEBD real-time dynamics of the Ising chain (quench light-cone, matched to exact), and full two-site DMRG — Matrix Product Operators + incremental environments + a matrix-free Lanczos eigensolver finding the ground state of the Ising and Heisenberg/XXZ chains, matching exact diagonalisation to ~1e-13 with a vanishing ⟨H²⟩−⟨H⟩² eigenstate certificate at lengths far past exact diag, plus a phase-transition scan that locates quantum critical points from the ground-state entanglement peak. And now TOPOLOGICAL ERROR CORRECTION: the rotated surface code [[d²,1,d]] with a real Minimum-Weight Perfect Matching decoder — a faithful from-scratch implementation of Edmonds' blossom algorithm (Galil's O(V³) primal-dual matching, verified against brute force) running on a graph of lit-up parity checks weighted by lattice distance. Inject X/Z errors on an interactive lattice and watch the checks fire, the matching form, and the residual be classified as a stabilizer or a logical failure; a Monte-Carlo sweep reproduces the ≈10.3% error-correction threshold where the distance-3/5/7 curves cross. Also: Kraus noise, a complex Hermitian eigensolver, Bloch spheres, Schmidt decomposition, error-correcting codes (bit/phase-flip, 9-qubit Shor, Steane [[7,1,3]] with live syndrome decoding), phase estimation, VQE with analytic parameter-shift gradients & QAOA, randomized benchmarking, OpenQASM export, and a 59-case self-test suite cross-checking every engine against exact references.
